text.title: Audience to Paid Conversion Loop Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Audience to Paid Conversion Loop methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Attribution window MUST be declared upfront (e.g. 7-day click, 1-day view) and frozen for the duration of the test. Adjusting after viewing data is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Adjusting window after seeing data manufactures wins; pre-declared windows make readouts falsifiable.

rule.statement: Each loop cycle changes exactly ONE variable (subject line, landing copy, price, CTA). Multi-variable changes are forbidden — they make causation un-attributable.
rule.rationale: Multi-variate at solo scale (small N) confuses signal with noise.

rule.statement: Cohort size MUST be ≥50 for any decision to be 'directional'; ≥200 for any decision to be 'kept'. <50 = log only, no decision.
rule.rationale: Below 50, day-of-week + outlier noise drowns signal.

rule.statement: LoopRunArtefact names a single owner. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
Decision (keep / drop / iterate) MUST be recorded only after the full attribution window has elapsed. Premature decisions invalidate the cycle.
rule.rationale: Truncated windows over-credit the variant that converts fast and under-credit slower converters.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-audience-to-paid-conversion-loop.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
